name: Nam Prik Curry Noodles (Kanom Jin Nam Prik)
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Morning glory (water spinach), roughly chopped
1/2 cup Dried split moong beans, soaked in water for 6 hours
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp (30 ml) of oil until a light haze forms. Fry half the shallots and half the garlic until crispy brown. Drain and set aside.
2. Drain the moong beans and pound them well to form a paste. In the mortar, pound together the coriander roots with the remaining shallots and garlic until a paste forms.
3. Heat the remaining oil in a frying pan or wok. Stir in the red curry paste and cook. Add half the coconut milk and stir well. Add the coriander root, shallot, and garlic paste, then stir thoroughly.
4. Add the moong bean paste and stir well. Add soy sauce, sugar, tamarind juice, lemon juice, and chili powder. Stir constantly. Add the kaffir lime halves and continue to cook gently.
5. Thin the remaining coconut milk with water, then stir into the sauce. Simmer for 1 minute.
6. Quickly stir in the crispy shallot and garlic mix and transfer to a serving bowl. Save the remaining oil for the side dish.
7. For the fried dry chilis: Reheat the oil and quickly stir-fry the chilis. Serve in a small bowl. Serves three.
